mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity cash flows and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a broad-based collapse in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint negative growth in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day SPX direction with a rank IC exceeding 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during identified recovery regimes, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in stronger economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX direction across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in >58% of periods following CPIAUCSL monthly increases above 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day SPX direction with a hit rate above 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle regimes does not predict negative 5-day SPX direction with a rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
